provenance.source_corpus: Managing conservation values and tree performance: Lessons learned from 10 year experiments in regenerating eastern white pine (Pinus strobus L.)

source_spans.text: 1-yr herb and woody suppression
favoured rapid white pine growth
increased pine canopy cover
allowed the understory community to recover
10-yr woody-only suppression
Pteridium aquilinum cover was greatest in the no suppression and 10-yr woody-only treatments
4-yr herb-only suppression
dominated by competitive trees and tall shrubs
4-yr herb and woody suppression
stem diameters were more than double those of the no suppression treatment
10-yr woody-only suppression... low functional diversity
4-yr herb-only suppression... highest functional diversity

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:f38f5e5ba01c
edges.original_sentence: Our results show that after 10years, plots treated with a single, second-season application of herbicide that temporarily suppressed both woody and herbaceous vegetation favoured rapid white pine growth, increased pine canopy cover, and allowed the understory community to recover towards a mature functional and taxonomic composition.

source_spans.text: Our results show that after 10years, plots treated with a single, second-season application of herbicide that temporarily suppressed both woody and herbaceous vegetation favoured rapid white pine growth, increased pine canopy cover, and allowed the understory community to recover towards a mature functional and taxonomic composition.

source_document.title: Managing conservation values and tree performance: Lessons learned from 10 year experiments in regenerating eastern white pine (Pinus strobus L.)

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:4758f022d62c
edges.original_sentence: In the present study we also found high taxonomic diversity but low functional diversity following 10-yr woody-only suppression in the shelterwood sites
